setup: CMRS = Caron-Huot, Mazac, Rastelli, Simmons-Duffin, arXiv:2102.08951 (v2, May 2021; JHEP 07 (2021) 110). EXTERNAL STATES: 2->2 scattering of IDENTICAL MASSLESS REAL SCALARS coupled to gravity, tree-level/weakly-coupled EFT (all couplings O(eps)), in asymptotically flat D-dim spacetime. AMPLITUDE eq (1) [their eq 2.1]: M_low(s,u) = 8piG[st/u + su/t + tu/s] - lambda_3^2[1/s + 1/t + 1/u] - lambda_4 + g_2(s^2+t^2+u^2) + g_3(stu) + g_4(s^2+t^2+u^2)^2 + ..., with s+t+u=0 (eq 2). NORMALIZATION: Newton constant enters ONLY as 8piG (no kappa^2 anywhere); lambda_3 = cubic scalar coupling (enters squared, with MINUS sign), lambda_4 = quartic constant; g_k multiply the crossing-symmetric monomial basis A^a B^b, A=s^2+t^2+u^2, B=stu, spin J=2a+2b, 'scaling dimension' 2k=4a+6b (subscript k = half the scaling dim; degenerate labels g_6, g_6' beyond k=5) (eqs 3-4). CRITICAL: lambda_3, lambda_4 (and any spin-0/spin-'<k' contact) drop out of ALL C_{k>=2} sum rules — selection rule eq (24): only contacts with J=2a+2b>=k and b<=k/2 contribute; the graviton enters ONLY C_2 via -8piG/u (text after eq 23). PARTIAL WAVES (identical to CHVD 2011.02957): M = s^{(4-D)/2} sum_{J even} n_J^{(D)} c_J(s) P_J(1+2u/s), P_J(x)=2F1(-J, J+D-3, (D-2)/2, (1-x)/2) (eq 9), n_J^{(D)} = 2^D pi^{(D-2)/2}/Gamma((D-2)/2) * (J+1)_{D-4} (2J+D-3) (eq 10); unitarity 0<=rho_J<=2 (eq 13), only positivity used. HEAVY AVERAGE eq (18): <(...)> = (1/pi) sum_{J even} n_J^{(D)} int_{M^2}^inf (dm^2/m^2) m^{4-D} rho_J(m^2) (...). ASSUMPTIONS: (i) analyticity in upper-half s-plane at fixed u<0; (ii) Regge spin-2 boundedness lim |M(s,u)/s^2| -> 0 along any constant-phase ray, fixed u<0 (eq 14) — postulated nonperturbatively (stronger than CRG conjecture); gap: M = mass of first state outside the EFT, rho_J=0 for 0<s<M^2. SUM RULES: C_{k,u} = oint ds'/(2pi i s') M(s',u)/[s'(s'+u)]^{k/2} = 0 (eq 22), giving C_{k,u} = Res_{s'=0}[(2s'+u)/(s'(s'+u)) * M_low/[s'(s'+u)]^{k/2}] - <(2m^2+u)/(m^2+u) * P_J(1+2u/m^2)/[m^2(m^2+u)]^{k/2}> (eq 23) — EXACTLY your validated CHVD B_k machinery with the fixed transfer named u instead of t (subtraction points s'=0, s'=-u preserve s<->t crossing). C_2 sum rule eq (20): 8piG/(-u) + 2g_2 - g_3 u + 8g_4 u^2 + ... = <(2m^2+u)P_J(1+2u/m^2)/(m^2(m^2+u)^2)>. KEY NEW OBJECT, improved sum rule eqs (42)-(44): C_2^improved_u = C_{2,u} - sum_{n>=2}(n u^{2n-2} C_{2n,0} + u^{2n-1} C'_{2n,0}); low-energy side EXACTLY 8piG/(-u) + 2g_2 - g_3 u (eq 43, no higher-contact contamination); heavy block (eq 44): C_2^imp[m^2,J](u) = (2m^2+u)P_J(1+2u/m^2)/(m^2(m^2+u)^2) - (u^2/m^6)[(4m^2+3u)P_J(1)/(m^2+u)^2 + 4u P'_J(1)/(m^4-u^2)], valid for u in (-M^2, 0]. Null constraints X_{k,u}[m^2,J] eq (51) (CHVD's crossing nulls, u-smeared). LP eq (46)-(47),(52): find f(p), h_k(p) on p in [0,M] (u=-p^2) with int f C_2^imp + sum_k int h_k X_k >= 0 for all m>=M, J=0,2,4,..., AND in the scaling limit m->inf, b=2J/m fixed (Bessel limit eq 48-49); conclusion int_0^M dp f(p)[8piG/p^2 + 2g_2 + g_3 p^2] >= 0. Impact-parameter transform eq (37): fhat(b) = Gamma((D-2)/2) int dp f(p) J_{(D-4)/2}(pb)/(pb/2)^{(D-4)/2}; graviton action eq (38): int dp f 8piG/p^2 = int db fhat(b) 8piG b/(D-4). C_4^improved eq (57) isolates 4g_4 - 2u g_5 + u^2 g_6'. SUSY sector (Sec 3.8): M_susy = 8piG/(stu) + g_0 + g_2(...) with s^2 M -> 0 Regge (eq 72); C_{-2}: 8piG/(-u) = <m^2(2m^2+u)P_J(1+2u/m^2)> (eq 73); C_0^improved: g_0 = <(2m^2+u)P_J(1+2u/m^2)/(m^2+u) - 2u^2/(m^4-u^2)> (eq 77). DIMENSIONS OF QUANTITATIVE RESULTS: headline g_2-g_3 exclusion in D=5,...,12 (Fig 4, Table 3); all detailed studies (Figs 3,5,6,7,9,10) in D=6; maximal sugra in D=10; D=4 only via leading-log IR regulator (Sec 3.7).

targets.name: No-gravity forward-limit ladder (machinery regression, G=0)
targets.statement: g_2 = <1/m^4> > 0; g_3/g_2 <= 3/M^2 (eq 31, elementary); -kappa(D)/M^2 < g_3/g_2 from J=2,4 convex hull with the single null constraint <J^2(2J^2-5D+4)/m^8>=0 (eqs 27, 32-35); 0 <= g_4/g_2 <= 1/(2M^4) (eq 36, exact).
targets.value: kappa(D) = sqrt[(D+3)(319D^3+76D^2-292D+32)/(24(D-2)^2(D+1)(D+4))] + 6(5D-2)/(12(D-2)); numerically kappa(4)=10.612, kappa(5)=9.094, kappa(6)=8.339, kappa(10)=7.218. Consistent with (slightly weaker than) your validated CHVD gt_3 ladder value -10.3466 in D=4.
targets.difficulty: Fixed-t/forward-limit only, zero new machinery — closed-form check of the D-dimensional Gegenbauer normalization before touching gravity.

targets.name: Non-gravitational g_3-g_4 region via smeared wavepackets == forward-limit region (D=6)
targets.statement: The (g_3 M^2/g_2, g_4 M^4/g_2) allowed region computed with small-impact-parameter wavepackets (17-dim functional space of Table 2 col 1 + C_{4,u=0}) is essentially identical to the CHVD forward-limit region (33-dim functional space).
targets.value: Region overlap 'essentially identical' (no number given; the CHVD D=6 region is the reference).
targets.difficulty: First smearing test: needs the p-basis, Bessel scaling constraint and adaptive refinement, but validated against a region you can compute with existing CHVD machinery. Recommended as the bridge target.

targets.name: HEADLINE: g_2-g_3 exclusion with gravity, D=5..12 (Table 3 inequalities)
targets.statement: In units 8piG=1, M=1, the allowed (g_2, g_3) region is the intersection of explicit linear inequalities g_2 + a g_3 + c >= 0. The first inequality in each D is the exact slope-3 edge g_2 - g_3/3 + c_0(D) >= 0.
targets.value: c_0(D) = 60.3086 (D=5), 28.0546 (D=6), 18.0717 (D=7), 13.7186 (D=8), 11.1250 (D=9), 9.5208 (D=10), 8.4687 (D=11), 7.9016 (D=12); full inequality lists (17/11/7/13/5/3/7/3 constraints) printed verbatim in Table 3. Implied polygon tip (min g_2 M^2/(8piG), computed from Table 3): -17.886 (D=5), -9.731 (D=6), -6.913 (D=7), -5.605 (D=8), -4.794 (D=9), -4.251 (D=10), -3.887 (D=11), -3.691 (D=12). These are THE machine-checkable numbers: reproduce any single Table 3 inequality (e.g. D=6, g_2 + 0.0940931 g_3 + 4.55838 >= 0) to ~1e-4.
targets.citation: Sec 3.4, Figure 4; Appendix A.3, Tables 2 and 3
targets.difficulty: Full machinery at its SMALLEST setting: 17 functionals ({p^n C_2^imp, n from Table 1 basis, n<=7.5} + {p^i X_4, i=0..5} + {p^i X_6, i=0..3}), J_max=42, plus the fixed-b scaling constraint and adaptive refinement; SDPB.

targets.name: Refined minimum g_2 and extremal spectrum (D=6)
targets.statement: Minimizing g_2 with a 92-dim functional space (93-dim projected to kill g_3) gives the extremal value and a spectrum with a single state at J=2,4,6 and nearly-flat leading trajectory (lower triangle m^2 <~ M^2 J/2, opposite to string theory).
targets.value: g_2 M^2/(8piG) = -9.57 (D=6, 92 functionals; Fig 7 caption quotes the tip as approx -9.6; 17-functional Table 3 value is -9.73). Paper states Fig-4-level bounds are converged to 1e-4 in cutoffs and 'within a few percent' of the optimum of LP (52).
targets.difficulty: Full machinery at large setting (J_max=150, 92 functionals up to X_14, extremal-functional zero extraction).

targets.name: Higher contacts g_4, g_5, g_6' with gravity (D=6)
targets.statement: Using C_4^improved (eq 57) alongside C_2^improved and nulls, (g_4, g_5, g_6') lie in compact 3d regions at fixed g_2; regions computed for g_2=0, g_2 M^2/(8piG)=-5, -9; as g_2 -> min (approx -9.6) the region shrinks to a point.
targets.value: Tip point: (g_4 M^6/(8piG), g_5 M^8/(8piG), g_6' M^10/(8piG)) approx (1.4, -34, 165). Functionals = Table 2 col 1 setup + {p^i C_4^imp, i=0..5}, restricted to the g_3-independent subspace.
targets.difficulty: Full machinery + second improved sum rule; only approximate (2-sig-fig) target values, read off a caption.

targets.name: Maximal supergravity R^4 bound (D=10) — SIMPLEST gravity-inclusive number
targets.statement: For the crossing-symmetric susy-reduced amplitude 8piG/(stu) + g_0 + ..., with spin-(-2) Regge improvement: 0 <= g_0 <= 3.000 * 8piG/M^6.
targets.value: Upper bound coefficient 3.000 (eq 78); obtained with only 14 functionals: C_{-2} (eq 73) smeared against p^2,...,p^10 (9 fns), C_0^improved (eq 77) against 4 powers of p, X_2 against 2 powers. String check: type II has g_0 M^6/(8piG) = 2 zeta(3) approx 2.4041 <= 3.000 (eq 79).
targets.difficulty: Smearing needed but by far the simplest gravitational LP (single coupling, gravity decoupled into its own sum rule, no C_2^imp block, 14 functionals). Best FIRST gravity target.

targets.name: D=4 leading-log bound
targets.statement: With positivity of fhat(b) imposed only for b < b_max (IR cutoff), replacing the gravity action by the log coefficient: g_2 >= -(8piG/M^2) * 25 * log(0.3 M b_max).
targets.value: Coefficients 25 and 0.3 (eq 70). WARNING: arXiv v1 of this equation is numerically WRONG (incorrect p_min <-> b_max relation, footnote 15); use v2/JHEP.
targets.difficulty: Full machinery + leading-log IR regulator scheme; scheme-dependent, lowest priority for exact reproduction.

targets.name: String-theory reference points (no SDP; analytic sanity anchors inside the bounds)
targets.statement: Type II dilaton Virasoro-Shapiro-like amplitude eq (58) has g_2=g_3=0, g_4 = 4piG zeta(3)/m^6, i.e. g_4 M^6/(8piG) = x zeta(3)/2, x=(M/m)^6 in (0,1] (eqs 60-62). Subtracting all spin-0 exchanges, in D=6: g_2 m^2/(8piG) approx -0.75, g_3 m^4/(8piG) approx -2.07 (eq 63). Heterotic dilatons: g_2 m^2/(8piG) = 3/16, g_3 m^4/(8piG) = 3/4 (eq 64).
targets.value: See statement; all must lie strictly inside the Table 3 region (they are far from saturation).
targets.difficulty: Pure low-energy expansion of a known amplitude; good end-to-end consistency check of your amplitude/coupling normalizations.

parameters: UNITS: everything solved with M=1, 8piG=1 (Appendix A, eq 80-81); u-domain u in [-M^2, 0], i.e. p in [0,1], u=-p^2 (Appendix B explores u_min/M^2 = -1, -5/4, -5/3, -5/2 with a 54-dim space — meromorphic amplitudes only). SMEARING BASIS (Table 1, chosen so the large-b tail can be made positive AND int f(p)/p^2 converges, requiring powers n>1): D=5: {p^3-p^2, p^4-p^2, p^5-p^2, ...}; even D>=6: half-integer powers {p^{3/2}, p^{5/2}, p^{7/2}, ...}; odd D>=7: {p^2, p^3, p^4, ...}. h_k(p) in nonnegative integer powers p^0, p^1, .... All p-integrals of the blocks are closed-form 2F1's (e.g. eq 83 for J=2). FIGURE 4 / TABLE 3 SETTINGS (Table 2, col 1): functionals {p^n C_2^imp | n<=7.5 in the Table 1 basis} + {p^i X_4 | i=0..5} + {p^i X_6 | i=0..3} = 17-dimensional; J_max=42 (verified: raising J_max changes bounds by < 1e-4 fractionally); heavy-mass grid via x with m^2=1/(1-x), x in {0, dx, 2dx, ...}, dx=1/400; scaling-limit (m->inf, b=2J/m fixed) constraint imposed explicitly via Bessel action eq (85)-(86): discretized b in {eps_b, eps_b+db, ...} with eps_b=1/250, db=1/32 up to B=40, and for b>B a rigorous 2x2 PSD-matrix condition (eqs 88-90) on the asymptotic expansion A(b) +/- B(b), C(b) keeping m_max=2 subleading tail terms (this is how positivity in (m^2,J) incl. J->inf is enforced). ADAPTIVE REFINEMENT (Appendix A.2, essential for soundness of the discretization): after each SDPB solve, locate negative dips between grid points (they ALWAYS occur), insert ~2N+1 points at spacing |x2-x1|/N with N=10 around each estimated minimum, re-solve from scratch; iterate until dips shrink to ~1e-6 (example in Fig 8: final negative region width 2.2e-6 in b, depth -1.83e-10). Bound extraction for Fig 4: maximize distance from a point near the tip along 40 rays theta in {0, pi/20, ..., 39pi/20}. FIGURE 6 SETTINGS (Table 2, col 2): functionals {p^n C_2^imp | n=1.5, 2.5, ..., 27.5} + {p^i X_4 | i=0..15} + {p^i X_6 | i=0..13} + ... + {p^i X_14 | i=0..5} = 93-dim, projected to the 92-dim g_3-blind subspace; J_max=150; dx=1/800; eps_b=1/250, db=1/100, B=80, m_max=6; extra x-constraints at x in {1-4/J, 1-4/J+1/(50J), ..., 1-1/(50J)} for J=36..150. SUGRA (Sec 3.8): 14 functionals as listed in the targets. SOLVER: SDPB (Simmons-Duffin 2015; Landry-Simmons-Duffin scalable version); non-default options: --precision=768 (Fig 4) / 840 (Fig 6), --dualityGapThreshold=1e-80, --primalErrorThreshold=1e-80, --dualErrorThreshold=1e-80. Functional tables generated in Mathematica at 300 decimal digits. PRECISION CLAIMS: Table 3 numbers quoted to 6 digits; bounds converged to fractional 1e-4 w.r.t. cutoffs; claimed within a few percent of the true optimum of LP (52). NO code or data ancillary files on arXiv — Table 3 IS the published data.
gotchas: Variable naming: 2102.08951 holds u fixed (t = -s-u eliminated); CHVD 2011.02957 holds t fixed. Your g-side residue Res_{s=0}[(2s+t)/(s(s+t)) M_low/[s(s+t)]^{k/2}] is literally their eq (23) with t->u. Keep one convention or you will silently flip g_3-odd signs.

lambda_3^2 and lambda_4 NEVER appear in any C_{k>=2} sum rule (selection rule eq 24: contact spin J>=k, b<=k/2; massless s/t/u scalar poles cancel between channels after the symmetric subtraction). They are unconstrained — do not expect them in validation targets. Note the MINUS signs in eq (1): -lambda_3^2[...] - lambda_4.
The graviton appears ONLY in C_2 (as -8piG/u on the low side); C_4, C_6, ... and all X_k are gravity-blind. In the SUSY problem gravity instead sits alone in C_{-2}.

Low spins with the pole: no special-casing needed beyond the block itself. J=0: P'_0(1)=0, and C_2^imp[m^2,0](u=-p^2) = 2/m^4 + 3p^2/m^6 EXACTLY (eq 56) — same functional form as the EFT side 2g_2+g_3p^2, which is what forces the exact slope-3 upper edge g_2 - g_3/3 + c_0 >= 0 (heavy scalars translate (g_2,g_3) by multiples of (1, 3/m^2)). Reproducing slope exactly 3 is a free structural check. P'_J(1) = J(J+D-3)/(D-2) enters the improvement terms for J>=2.

D=4 is EXCLUDED from all sharp results: the b-space transform of 8piG/p^2 is log-divergent (int d^2b fhat > 0 forced by positivity vs f(p)/p -> 0 needed for convergence). Only the leading-log bound eq (70) exists, and arXiv v1's version of it is numerically WRONG (footnote 15) — cite/use v2 (May 2021) = JHEP 07 (2021) 110. All D>4 results are IR-clean.

Basis powers must satisfy n>1 or the gravity action int f(p)/p^2 diverges; additionally the large-b tail (eq 87) forces D-dependent bases: D=5 is the awkward case (must use differences p^k - p^2 to cancel the leading oscillation), even D use half-integer powers p^{3/2}+..., odd D>=7 plain integers. A generic power choice gives functionals that CANNOT be positive at large b.

You must impose the m->inf, b=2J/m fixed scaling constraint (Bessel positivity, eq 85) as an explicit extra constraint family — otherwise convergence in J_max is bad; and the b>B tail must be handled by the 2x2 PSD trick (eq 90), not by truncation.

Naive grid discretization in x (or b) is UNSOUND: SDPB solutions generically dip negative between grid points. The published bounds are only correct because of the adaptive-refinement/outer-approximation loop (Appendix A.2). Your exact-arithmetic a-posteriori audit is the right countermeasure — but expect the extremal functional to have near-zero dips of depth ~1e-10 that the audit must resolve (Sturm at high precision).

Improved sum rule range: C_2^imp is only justified for u in (-M^2, 0]; at u=-M^2 the t-channel cut hits the origin. Appendix B's extended-range bounds (kink slides, tip moves toward -8.7-ish region, spectra simplify toward m^2 = M^2(J-2)/2) assume meromorphy — NOT part of the rigorous headline and not a validation target.
Only even J in the partial-wave sum (identical real scalars); heavy-average measure eq (18) has the m^{4-D} dm^2/m^2 weight — identical to CHVD, so your n_J^{(D)}/measure code carries over unchanged.

Table 3 tips vs Fig 6: min g_2 M^2/(8piG) in D=6 is -9.731 from the 17-functional Table 3 polygon but -9.57 from the 92-functional run (Fig 7 caption rounds to -9.6). When validating, match the functional space to the number: Table 3 inequalities at Table-2-col-1 settings should reproduce to ~1e-4; the -9.57 needs the big col-2 setup.
Bounds strengthen monotonically with D (dimensional reduction positivity of Gegenbauers) — a useful cross-D sanity monotonicity check on c_0(D) and the tips.
g_2 M^2/(8piG) has NO upper bound (g_2 and G independent); only lower bounds and compactness of g_3, g_4, ... at fixed g_2. Don't search for a two-sided g_2 bound.

kappa(D) as rendered in ar5iv (eq 35) contains the unreduced fraction 6(5D-2)/(12(D-2)) = (5D-2)/(2(D-2)); verify against kappa(4) approx 10.612 (must be slightly weaker than the CHVD multi-functional value 10.3466 you already reproduce).
